articles.headline: Janelle Salaun scores 21 points off the bench and Valkyries beat Mercury 95-79 to win home opener
articles.description: — Janelle Salaun scored 21 points off the bench, Gabby Williams contributed 19 points and five rebounds in her home debut for Golden State after the All-Star forward signed last month, and the Valkyries beat the Phoenix Mercury 95-79 on Sunday night.

articles.headline: Aces rebound from season-opening drubbing with win vs. Sparks
articles.description: In a span of 24 hours, the Aces received their championship rings, suffered a historically bad season-opening loss and then picked up their first win over the Sparks on Sunday.

articles.headline: Carter scores 22, Young adds 20 as Aces beat Sparks 105-78
articles.description: — Chennedy Carter scored 22 points, Jackie Young had 20 points and nine assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat Los Angeles 105-78 on Sunday in the Sparks' season opener.

articles.headline: Johannes scores 25, Stewart adds 23 and 9 rebounds as Liberty beat Mystics 98-93 in OT
articles.description: — Marine Johannes hit six 3-pointers and scored 25 points, Breanna Stewart had 23 points and nine rebounds, and the New York Liberty beat the Washington Mystics 98-93 in overtime on Sunday.

articles.headline: Lexie Brown hits 5 3-pointers and scores 17 as the Storm beat the Sun 89-82
articles.description: — Lexie Brown hit five 3-pointers and scored 17 points, Flau’jae Johnson had 16 and the Seattle Storm beat the Connecticut Sun 89-82 on Sunday.

article.headline: Austin scores 19, Mystics finish strong to hand Lynx second loss 68-64
article.description: — Shakira Austin scored 19 points and the Washington Mystics scored the last six points of the game to hand the Minnesota Lynx, without All-Star Napheesa Collier, their second loss, 68-64 on Tuesday night.
article.linkText: Austin scores 19, Mystics finish strong to hand Lynx second loss 68-64

images.name: Washington Mystics vs. Minnesota Lynx - Game Highlights
images.caption: Watch the Game Highlights from Washington Mystics vs. Minnesota Lynx, 06/24/2025

article.story: WASHINGTON -- — Shakira Austin scored 19 points and the Washington Mystics scored the last six points of the game to hand the Minnesota Lynx, without All-Star Napheesa Collier, their second loss, 68-64 on Tuesday night. Austin's putback tied the game at 64 with 32 seconds left. After a timeout to advance the ball, Bridget Carleton's inbounds pass went off Courtney Williams' hands and Sug Sutton scooped up the loose ball and raced in for the winning basket. Sonia Citron secured the win with two free throws with 6.4 seconds remaining. Washington didn't make a basket after the 5:12 mark, when Kiki Iriafen’s short jumper on the baseline gave Washington a 62-61 lead until Austin’s tying basket. Sutton scored 12 points for the Mystics (7-8) and Kiki Iriafen added 11 points and 11 rebounds. Citron finished 3 of 13 from the field for nine points, ending her streak of double-figure games to start her career at 14. Alanna Smith scored a career-high 26 points for Minnesota (12-2) Jessica Shepard had 12 points and 15 rebounds. Collier missed her second straight game with a lower back injury. The Lynx, one of the top 3-point shooting teams, went 3 of 18 behind the arc and were just 17 of 25 from the line, going 6 of 12 after their last field goal, which game with 8:48 to play. They also had 19 turnovers the Mystics turned into 26 points. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
